content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Found 1 correctness regression. The new synthetic Codex defaults pin implementer/final-review reviewer models above `settings.default_model`, so an existing workspace/project override path no longer works. `cargo test`, `cargo fmt --check`, and `cargo clippy -q` all passed.

## Amendments

1. Preserve `default_model` overrides for the new Codex defaults. `default_backend_runtime_settings()` now hardcodes `backends.codex.role_models.implementer = gpt-5.4-high` and `backends.codex.role_models.final_reviewer = gpt-5.4-xhigh` ([src/contexts/workspace_governance/config.rs:1542-1547]), `EffectiveConfig::load` makes the implementer backend selection explicit by default ([src/contexts/workspace_governance/config.rs:427-435]), and `target_for_family()` always prefers `role_models` over `base_backend.model/default_model` ([src/contexts/agent_execution/policy.rs:327-340]). As a result, a workspace/project override like `default_backend = "codex"` plus `default_model = "override-model"` no longer changes the implementer or first final-review reviewer: `backend probe --role implementer` / `final_reviewer` still resolve to `gpt-5.4-high` / `gpt-5.4-xhigh`. That regresses the existing workspace/project override path the bead explicitly says must keep working.
   Rationale: The new defaults are encoded at a higher precedence than `settings.default_model`, so an established workspace/project config override silently stops working for the affected Codex roles. This is a behavioral regression, not just a diagnostics mismatch.
